title: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken

description: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a beloved dish featuring perfectly crispy chicken coated in a luscious, sweet, and spicy glaze. This iconic Korean comfort food offers an irresistible balance of flavors and textures, making it a favorite worldwide. Enjoy the delightful crunch followed by a burst of savory, tangy, and subtly fiery sauce.

ingredients.notes: such as vegetable or canola oil

instructions.title: Prepare and Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: Wash and thoroughly pat dry the chicken pieces. This is crucial for crispiness. In a large bowl, combine the chicken with 1 teaspoon salt, 1/2 teaspoon black pepper, 1 teaspoon garlic powder, 1 tablespoon soy sauce, and 1 tablespoon mirin. Mix well and let it marinate for at least 15 minutes at room temperature, or up to 30 minutes in the refrigerator.

instructions.title: Coat the Chicken for Frying
instructions.text: Add 1 cup of potato starch to the marinated chicken and toss until each piece is evenly coated. Ensure there are no dry spots; the starch should adhere well to the moist chicken.

instructions.title: First Fry the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 6 cups of frying oil in a deep pot or Dutch oven to 350F (175C). Carefully add chicken pieces, frying in batches to avoid overcrowding, for 6-8 minutes until light golden and cooked through. Remove and place on a wire rack to drain excess oil.

instructions.title: Prepare the Yangnyeom Sauce
instructions.text: While the chicken is frying, combine 1/4 cup gochujang, 1 tablespoon gochugaru (if using), 2 tablespoons soy sauce, 1/4 cup honey, 1 tablespoon rice vinegar, 2 cloves minced garlic, 1 teaspoon sesame oil, 2 tablespoons water, and 1 tablespoon brown sugar in a saucepan. Bring to a simmer over medium heat, stirring constantly until the sauce slightly thickens, about 3-5 minutes.

instructions.title: Second Fry for Extra Crispiness
instructions.text: Increase the oil temperature to 375F (190C). Return the first-fried chicken pieces to the hot oil for a second fry, in batches, for another 2-3 minutes until deep golden brown and extra crispy. This double-frying technique is key for the signature crunch.

instructions.title: Sauce and Serve
instructions.text: Immediately transfer the hot, double-fried chicken to a large bowl. Pour the prepared Yangnyeom sauce over the chicken and toss gently until all pieces are thoroughly coated. Arrange the sauced chicken on a serving platter. Garnish generously with toasted sesame seeds and chopped green onions. Serve hot and enjoy the crispy, sweet, and spicy delight.

tips: Double-frying is the secret to achieving that signature extra-crispy Korean fried chicken texture. Dont skip this step!
For the most authentic crunch, potato starch is highly recommended over cornstarch, as it creates a lighter, crispier coating.
Serve Yangnyeom chicken immediately after coating for the best texture. Garnish with plenty of toasted sesame seeds and fresh green onions for flavor and presentation.
article:
Get ready to embark on a culinary journey that will tantalize your taste buds and leave you craving more! Korean Fried Chicken, affectionately known as KFC (the other KFC!), has taken the world by storm, and at the heart of its irresistible appeal lies the iconic Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken. This isn't just any fried chicken; it's a masterpiece of crispy texture coated in a vibrant, sticky, sweet, and spicy sauce that defines a truly beloved Korean chicken recipe. Whether you're a long-time fan or new to the world of Korean cuisine, mastering this dish at home promises a deliciously rewarding experience.

## What is Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken?

Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a quintessential dish in Korean cuisine, celebrated for its unique combination of textures and flavors. "Yangnyeom" (양념) literally translates to "seasoned" or "sauce," and in this context, it refers to the signature sweet, spicy, and savory glaze that coats perfectly crispy fried chicken. Unlike traditional Western fried chicken, Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is almost always double-fried, a technique that renders the skin incredibly thin, light, and shatteringly crisp, even after being tossed in sauce. The sauce itself is a complex symphony of flavors, typically featuring gochujang (Korean chili paste) for heat and depth, balanced with sweetness from honey or corn syrup, tang from vinegar, and savory notes from soy sauce and garlic. This harmonious blend makes it one of the most sought-after Korean chicken recipes globally, adored for its addictive stickiness and fiery kick.

## Ingredients You Need

To create authentic Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken, you'll need a combination of fresh ingredients for the chicken and a few key Korean pantry staples for the sauce.

**For the Chicken:**
*   **Chicken:** 2-3 lbs chicken wings, drumsticks, or boneless chicken thighs, cut into bite-ized pieces if desired.
*   **Marinade/Seasoning:** 1 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp black pepper, 1 tsp garlic powder.
*   **Dry Batter:** 1 cup all-purpose flour, 1/2 cup potato starch or cornstarch (for extra crispiness).
*   **Wet Batter:** 1 large egg, 1/2 cup cold water (or soda water for a lighter batter).
*   **Frying Oil:** 4-6 cups neutral oil with a high smoke point (e.g., vegetable, canola, peanut oil).

**For the Yangnyeom Sauce:**
*   **Gochujang:** 1/4 cup Korean chili paste (essential for authentic flavor).
*   **Honey or Corn Syrup:** 1/4 cup (for sweetness and gloss).
*   **Soy Sauce:** 2 tbsp.
*   **Rice Vinegar:** 1 tbsp.
*   **Minced Garlic:** 2-3 cloves, finely minced.
*   **Minced Ginger:** 1 tsp, finely minced (optional, but adds depth).
*   **Sugar:** 1 tbsp (white or brown sugar, to balance the spice).
*   **Ketchup:** 1 tbsp (adds tang and umami, optional).
*   **Sesame Oil:** 1 tsp.

**For Garnish:**
*   Toasted sesame seeds.
*   Chopped green onions.

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Making Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken at home is a rewarding process that follows a few crucial steps for achieving that perfect crisp and flavorful coating.

1.  **Prepare the Chicken:** Pat the chicken pieces thoroughly dry with paper towels. This is a critical step to ensure crispiness. Season the chicken with salt, pepper, and garlic powder.
2.  **Make the Batter:** In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour and potato starch (or cornstarch). In a separate bowl, whisk the egg and cold water. Gradually add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients, mixing until just combined. A few lumps are okay; avoid overmixing. The batter should be thick enough to coat the chicken but not excessively clumpy.
3.  **Coat the Chicken:** Dip each seasoned chicken piece into the batter, ensuring it's evenly coated. Let any excess drip off.
4.  **First Fry:** Heat the frying oil in a large deep pot or Dutch oven to 325°F (160°C). Carefully add the coated chicken pieces in batches, ensuring not to overcrowd the pot. Fry for 6-8 minutes, or until lightly golden and cooked through. Remove the chicken and place it on a wire rack set over a baking sheet to drain.
5.  **Second Fry (The Key to Crispiness!):** Increase the oil temperature to 350-375°F (175-190°C). Return the partially fried chicken to the hot oil in batches. Fry for another 3-5 minutes, or until deeply golden brown and exceptionally crispy. This double-frying technique is what gives Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken its signature crunch. Remove and drain again on a wire rack.
6.  **Prepare the Yangnyeom Sauce:** While the chicken is frying, combine all the Yangnyeom sauce ingredients (gochujang, honey/corn syrup, soy sauce, rice vinegar, minced garlic, minced ginger, sugar, ketchup, and sesame oil) in a small saucepan. Heat over medium-low heat, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens and becomes glossy, about 3-5 minutes.
7.  **Sauce the Chicken:** In a large mixing bowl, add the freshly double-fried chicken. Pour the warm Yangnyeom sauce over the chicken and toss gently but quickly until every piece is evenly coated.
8.  **Garnish and Serve:** Transfer the sauced Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken to a serving platter. Sprinkle generously with toasted sesame seeds and chopped green onions. Serve immediately.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving that perfect Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken experience at home is all about attention to detail. Here are some pro tips to elevate your dish:

*   **Pat Your Chicken Dry:** Excess moisture is the enemy of crispy fried chicken. Always pat your chicken pieces thoroughly dry before seasoning and battering.
*   **Double Frying is Non-Negotiable:** This is the secret to the incredibly crispy, thin crust that defines authentic Korean fried chicken. The first fry cooks the chicken, and the second fry, at a higher temperature, renders the skin shatteringly crisp and locks in that crunch, even under the sauce.
*   **Maintain Oil Temperature:** Use a deep-fry thermometer to monitor and maintain the oil temperature. Too low, and your chicken will be greasy; too high, and it will burn before cooking through.
*   **Don't Overcrowd the Pot:** Frying in batches is essential. Overcrowding the fryer drops the oil temperature, leading to soggy, poorly cooked chicken.
*   **Adjust Sauce to Taste:** The Yangnyeom sauce recipe provides a balanced sweet and spicy flavor. Feel free to adjust the gochujang for more heat, or honey/sugar for more sweetness, to suit your personal preference.
*   **Toss Immediately:** Coat the chicken in the sauce as soon as it comes out of the second fry. The residual heat helps the sauce adhere and creates that irresistible glossy finish.

## Serving Suggestions

Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a star in its own right, but it truly shines when paired with traditional Korean accompaniments.

*   **Pickled Radish (Chicken Mu):** This is the quintessential side dish for Korean fried chicken. The crisp, tangy, and refreshing pickled radish provides a perfect counterpoint to the rich, spicy chicken.
*   **Steamed Rice:** A simple bowl of fluffy white rice is excellent for soaking up any extra sauce and balancing the intense flavors of the chicken.
*   **Kimchi:** No Korean meal is complete without kimchi. Its fermented, spicy, and sour notes offer another layer of flavor and texture.
*   **Cold Beer (Chimaek):** In Korea, fried chicken and beer (Chimaek, a portmanteau of "chicken" and "maekju" or beer) is a beloved combination. The crispness of a cold lager perfectly complements the rich chicken.
*   **Coleslaw or Salad:** A simple, refreshing coleslaw or a light green salad can also provide a nice contrast.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a masterpiece, there's always room for personal touches and dietary adjustments.

*   **Chicken Cuts:** While wings and drumsticks are popular, boneless chicken thighs or even chicken tenders work wonderfully for easier eating. Just adjust frying times accordingly.
*   **Spice Level:** For a milder version, reduce the amount of gochujang and gochugaru (if using). For an extra kick, add more gochugaru or a pinch of cayenne pepper to the sauce.
*   **Sweetness:** Adjust the amount of honey or corn syrup to your liking. Maple syrup can also be used as a substitute for a different kind of sweetness.
*   **Gluten-Free:** To make this a gluten-free Korean chicken recipe, use a gluten-free all-purpose flour blend for the batter and tamari instead of regular soy sauce for the sauce. Ensure your gochujang is also gluten-free, as some brands may contain wheat.
*   **Healthier Twist (Non-Traditional):** While deep-frying is traditional, you can try air-frying or baking the chicken for a lighter version. However, be aware that the crispiness won't be quite the same as double-fried.
*   **Vegetarian Option:** The Yangnyeom sauce can be used to coat crispy fried cauliflower florets or firm tofu for a delicious vegetarian alternative.

Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is more than just a meal; it's an experience. The meticulous double-frying process, combined with the explosive flavors of the sweet and spicy sauce, creates an unforgettable dish that exemplifies the best of Korean chicken recipes. Don't be intimidated by the steps; with a little practice, you'll be creating restaurant-quality Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken right in your own kitchen, ready to impress family and friends with this truly iconic Korean culinary delight.
